mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower the risk-free discount rate applied to equity valuations, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with FEDFUNDS declines.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate above 0.5 percentage points signals sudden labor market deterioration, triggering immediate risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances following a >0.5pp UNRATE rise, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and capital goods demand, driving corporate earnings revisions and positive S&P 500 returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint increases in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive 60-day returns, with the interaction term coefficient statistically insignificant (p > 0.10) in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production signal supply-side contraction that compresses corporate profit margins, generating negative S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon specifically during late-cycle expansions.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines produce positive or neutral 20-day forward returns during late-cycle expansions, or the predictive IC flips sign outside the specified regime.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth reflects robust aggregate demand that supports near-term corporate revenue expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show zero or negative correlation with 5-day forward SPX returns across multiple rolling 2-year windows, or Rank IC falls below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 220.0, inflation expectations become entrenched, forcing monetary authorities to maintain restrictive policy and compressing equity valuations over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: SPX delivers positive 60-day returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL remains above 220.0, or the threshold effect is statistically indistinguishable from noise.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a falling unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that boosts consumer spending power and drives positive S&P 500 returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising PAYEMS and falling UNRATE fails to generate positive 20-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales signals weakening consumer demand that immediately pressures equity prices, producing negative S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon during periods of elevated market volatility.
failure_condition: RSAFS declines do not predict negative 5-day SPX returns during high volatility periods, or the signal loses predictive power when market volatility indices exceed historical thresholds.
